metadata.title: Short-term fasting abolishes the sex-related difference in GH and leptin secretion in humans
metadata.demographics: Fifteen normal adult volunteers: eight men (age 29.3 ± 1.2 yr, BMI 22.2 ± 0.5 kg/m²) and seven women (age 28.0 ± 0.8 yr, BMI 20.1 ± 0.7 kg/m²)

evidence.quote: Short-term fasting abolishes the sex-related difference in GH and leptin secretion in humans
Am J Physiol Endocrinol Metab 279: E411–E416, 2000

We studied growth hormone (GH) and leptin secretion in eight male (age 29.3 6 1.2 yr, body mass index 22.2 6 0.5 kg/m2) and seven female normal subjects (28.0 6 0.8 yr, 20.1 6 0.7 kg/m2) before and after 36 h of fasting
Fifteen normal adult volunteers matched for age and weight[eight men, age 29.361.2yr, body mass index (BMI) 22.260.5kg/m2; and seven women, age 28.060.8yr, BMI 20.1 6 0.7 kg/m2] were studied
eight male (age 29.3 6 1.2 yr, body mass index 22.2 6 0.5 kg/m2)
seven female normal subjects (28.0 6 0.8 yr, 20.1 6 0.7 kg/m2)
eight male (age 29.3 6 1.2 yr
seven female normal subjects (28.0 6 0.8 yr
body mass index 22.2 6 0.5 kg/m2
before and after 36 h of fasting
Serum GH levels (mg/l) were measured in duplicate at each timepoint by both immunoradiometric assay [IRMA; human GH, CTK, Sorin, Saluggia, Italy]
and immunofunctional assay (IFA; in-house method, Neuroendocrine Unit laboratory, Munich, Germany)
Serum leptin levels (mg/l) were measured in duplicate at each timepoint by RIA using commercial kits (Human Leptin RIA, Linco Research, St. Charles, MO)
Serum insulin levels (mU/ml) were measured in duplicate by the RIA method (INSIK-5, Sorin)

Serum IGF-I levels (mg/l) were measured in duplicate by the RIA method (Nichols Institute of Diagnostics, San Juan Capistrano, CA) after acid-ethanol extraction to avoid interference by binding proteins
From 8 AM to 4 PM, blood samples were taken every 30 min from an antecubital vein
From 8 AM to 4 PM, blood samples were taken every 30 min
all subjects had a 200-kcal breakfast (60% carbohydrate) at 8 AM
an 800-kcal (50% carbohydrate, 30% lipid, 20% protein) lunch
All female subjects had regular menses and were studied in their early follicular phase
All subjects had had stable weight in the last 6 mo before entering the study protocol
were on a classical Mediterranean diet (≈50% carbohydrates, 30% lipids, and 20% proteins)

narrative_results.statement: Mean leptin concentration in men was lower than in women before fasting

evidence.quote: Mean leptin concentration in men (2.7 ± 0.5 mg/l) was lower (P < 0.0001) than in women (5.9 ± 0.5 mg/l; Figs. 2-3).

narrative_results.statement: Mean GH concentration in men was lower than in women before fasting when assayed by IRMA

evidence.quote: When assayed by IRMA, mean GH concentration in men (0.5 ± 0.4 mg/l) was lower (P < 0.05) than in women (2.3 ± 0.4 mg/l).

narrative_results.statement: After 36 h of fasting, IGF-I, IGFBP-1, and FFA increased independently of gender

evidence.quote: After 36 h of fasting, IGF-I, IGFBP-1, and FFA increased (P < 0.001) independently of gender, whereas IGFBP-3, ALS, and GHBP were unchanged (Fig. 1).

narrative_results.statement: Fasting increased GH and decreased leptin in both sexes with significant interaction between gender and fasting

evidence.quote: Fasting increased GH and decreased leptin in both sexes. There was significant interaction between gender and fasting (P < 0.05 for GH and P < 0.005 for leptin).

narrative_results.statement: Females showed a lower GH increase than males after fasting in terms of percent increase above baseline

evidence.quote: Females showed a lower GH increase (P < 0.05 in terms of percent increase above baseline) than males independently of the assay used (males vs. females: IRMA, 6.2 ± 1.1 vs. 5.4 ± 1.2 mg/l; IFA, 3.3 ± 0.7 vs. 2.7 ± 0.5 mg/l).

narrative_results.statement: Females showed a higher leptin decrease than males after fasting in terms of percent decrease below baseline

evidence.quote: On the other hand, females showed a higher leptin decrease (P < 0.05 in terms of percent decrease below baseline) than males (males vs. females: 0.3 ± 0.2 vs. 3.2 ± 0.5 mg/l, P < 0.001).

narrative_results.statement: Gender-related differences in GH and leptin levels are no longer detectable after fasting

evidence.quote: Thus our results demonstrate that the gender-related differences in GH and leptin levels in humans are no longer detectable after fasting.

narrative_results.statement: Short-term fasting stimulates GH less and inhibits leptin more in women than in men

evidence.quote: In fact, short-term fasting stimulates GH less and inhibits leptin more in women than in men before any decrease in total IGF-I, as well as in IGFBP-3 and ALS levels.

figures_graphs.caption: Fig. 1. Before (open bars) and after fasting (hatched bars), mean (and range) levels of insulin-like growth factor I

semantic_groups.rationale: Both report 7 female participants; minor wording differences in phrasing
Pass 1 contains male age data; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains female age data; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains male BMI data; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains female BMI data; Pass 2 is empty
Both report 36 hours fasting duration; minor formatting differences
Both identify GH assay method 1 as IRMA/immunoradiometric assay; same information with different ordering
Both identify GH assay method 2 as IFA/immunofunctional assay; same information with different ordering
Both describe leptin assay using RIA/radioimmunoassay; only capitalization and formatting differ
Both describe insulin assay using RIA/radioimmunoassay; only capitalization and formatting differ
Both describe IGF-I assay using RIA with acid-ethanol extraction; only capitalization and formatting differ
Both convey sampling every 30 minutes; pass_1 uses structured format while pass_2 uses prose
Both specify 8 hours sampling duration; only capitalization and formatting differ
Both confirm 200 kcal breakfast; pass_2 adds carbohydrate composition detail not in pass_1
Both confirm 800 kcal lunch; pass_2 adds macronutrient composition details not in pass_1
Both specify early follicular phase for female menstrual phase; only capitalization differs
Both describe weight stability requirement over 6 months; minor wording differences only
Both specify Mediterranean diet; pass_2 adds specific macronutrient composition details
Both report 8 male participants; negligible wording differences
